specs.reason: Message, Role, ToolCall, ToolResult, TokenUsage are fundamental types that protocols will model and reference
ChangeProtocol will model AnalystAgent state machine, CodingAgent configuration, and agent execution patterns
SessionState and SessionInfo are core types; ChangeProtocol extends these types as domain contracts
CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse, ToolDefinition are types that appear in protocol contracts
Tool trait, ToolRegistry, ToolDefinition, ToolParameter are essentials for tool-related protocol contracts
SecurityPolicy, ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse are security protocols and contracts that will be modeled
ContextManager, ContextStats model context management state that protocols will codify
Module dependency diagram shows how protocols fit into overall architecture; C4 context clarifies system boundaries
Issue, PlatformIntegration types inform SyncTarget and change state protocol design
StreamEvent types and state machine may be part of protocol contracts for streaming awareness
Tool input/output schemas (BashTool, ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, etc.) inform CodeIndex field design
Analysis tool types and schemas inform protocol field design for session state and findings
NovaError classification and error types are part of protocol result and response contracts

summary: Reference context covers cclab-agent's complete type system, agent execution patterns, and domain contracts needed to define agent-protocols-module. HIGH relevance specs provide core types and behaviors that protocols will model as pure contracts. MEDIUM relevance specs provide architectural context and supporting details for comprehensive protocol design.
